source: Median across 4 light-tier benchmark runs on dev-tip 9569712 (PR #101 ibot, #102 53x9, #103 7nmw, #105 k7jk — all produced bit-identical predictions due to the per-sha NFS staging bug fixed in beefcake-swarm PR #272). The aitchison values are the actual unified-runner outputs from cflibs/benchmark/unified.py composition_records.json grouped by dataset_id. Per-dataset Stark-aware light-tier iterative_jax solver with id_workflow_name in {alias, comb}.
notes: Schema-corrected baseline (2026-05-09): the prior bootstrap baseline keyed entries by 'pipeline:HUBER+ilr' etc., but the actual unified runner emits records keyed by dataset_id ('aalto_libs', 'aa1100_substrate', ...). Since baseline.get(dataset_id) returned None, the per-dataset regression check in python/benchmark_gate.py:build_composition_delta silently classified +722% cross-dataset regressions as 'flat'. PR #272 (beefcake-swarm) adds a cross-dataset regression catch as defense-in-depth; this baseline file fixes the data layer so per-dataset regression detection works correctly. Initial baseline values are pathologically high (d_A 19-46) — they reflect the algorithm's CURRENT performance on hard mixture spectra (aa1100 is Al alloy, aalto is multi-element). Future architect improvements that LOWER d_A will register as 'improved'. The bayesian workflow on aalto_libs achieves d_A=9.48 (vs iterative_jax 19.54), demonstrating the gate IS sensitive to workflow choice once code paths are isolated.
